req.title: Open checking account (idempotency variant)
req.statement: A verified customer shall be able to open a checking account with an initial deposit between $25 and $50,000; deposits outside this range shall be rejected. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines three orthogonal concerns: (1) valid deposit range [25, 50000] accepting mid-range and exact-boundary values, (2) rejection of out-of-range deposits with HTTP 422, (3) rejection of unverified customers with HTTP 403, and (4) idempotency on duplicate Idempotency-Key submissions. I apply EP to confirm the happy path with a mid-range deposit, BVA to probe the exact lower bound (25) and upper bound (50000) plus their immediate neighbours (24, 50001), NEG for unverified customer and out-of-range deposits, DT to cover the combined condition matrix (verified×in-range), and STC to model the state change from no-account → account-open and the idempotent re-submission leaving state unchanged. Six tests are generated: positive mid-range EP, boundary lower-exact BVA, boundary upper-exact BVA, negative below-minimum NEG, negative unverified-customer NEG, and idempotency STC.

tests.name: test_open_checking_account_valid_mid_range_deposit
actions: POST /accounts/checking with verified customer credentials and initial_deposit=5000
Capture HTTP response status and body
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_open_checking_account_valid_mid_range_deposit(verified_customer_client, account_cleanup):
    payload = {"initial_deposit": 5000, "account_type": "checking"}
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": <generate_idempotency_key>()}

    resp = verified_customer_client.post("/accounts/checking", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "account_id" in body
    assert body.get("status") == "active"
    assert body.get("notification_sent") is True
    account_cleanup(body["account_id"])

tests.name: test_open_checking_account_unverified_customer_forbidden
preconditions: initial_deposit=5000 (valid range, isolating the unverified-customer condition)
actions: POST /accounts/checking with unverified customer credentials and initial_deposit=5000
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains error indicating customer not verified
trace: R-FIN-076: error pathway 403 when customer not verified

tests.name: test_open_checking_account_idempotent_duplicate_submission
preconditions: A unique Idempotency-Key is held constant across both requests
actions: POST /accounts/checking with verified customer credentials, initial_deposit=1000, and Idempotency-Key=<fixed_key>
Capture account_id and full response body from first request
POST /accounts/checking with identical payload and the same Idempotency-Key=<fixed_key>
Capture response body from second request
expected: First request returns HTTP 201 with account_id
Second request returns HTTP 200 or 201 (idempotent replay)
No duplicate account is created in the system
trace: R-FIN-076: duplicate submission with same Idempotency-Key must return original result
STC: state transition no-account → account-open (first call); account-open → account-open (idempotent replay)
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_open_checking_account_idempotent_duplicate_submission(verified_customer_client, account_cleanup):
    idempotency_key = <generate_idempotency_key>()
    payload = {"initial_deposit": 1000, "account_type": "checking"}
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}

    resp1 = verified_customer_client.post("/accounts/checking", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp1.status_code == 201
    body1 = resp1.json()
    account_id = body1["account_id"]

    resp2 = verified_customer_client.post("/accounts/checking", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp2.status_code in (200, 201)
    body2 = resp2.json()

    assert body2["account_id"] == account_id
    account_count = <get_account_count_by_idempotency_key>(idempotency_key)
    assert account_count == 1
    account_cleanup(account_id)
